title: revisions data model and migration
description: Add the `revisions` table (`id, page_id, snapshot_json, autor_id, criado_em, mensagem?`) to the Drizzle schema, storing a full snapshot of a page's content at publish time.
acceptanceCriteria: `revisions` table exists with `id, page_id (fk -> pages.id), snapshot_json (text), autor_id (fk -> users.id), criado_em (timestamp), mensagem (text, nullable)`
`snapshot_json` is documented to store the full content of every tab belonging to the page at the time of publish (all blocks across all tabs), not just one tab
Migration applies cleanly on top of prior migrations
Deleting a page cascades to its revisions (consistent with other cascade decisions)

steps.details: Define `revisions` with `id`, `pageId: text('page_id').notNull().references(() => pages.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' })`, `snapshotJson: text('snapshot_json').notNull()`, `autorId: text('autor_id').notNull().references(() => users.id)`, `criadoEm: integer('criado_em', { mode: 'timestamp' }).notNull()`, `mensagem: text('mensagem')`.

In `packages/schema`, define a `PageSnapshot` type: `{ tabs: { tabId: string; titulo: string; blocks: Block[] }[] }`, capturing all tabs of the page as of publish time — this is what gets serialized into `snapshot_json`.
Run `drizzle-kit generate` and confirm the foreign keys and cascade-on-delete-page behavior.
Insert a sample revision with a two-tab snapshot, read it back, and confirm `JSON.parse(snapshot_json)` matches the `PageSnapshot` type.

technicalNotes: A revision snapshots the whole page (all its tabs), not a single tab, because 'Publicar' is a page-level action per the PRD's user flow ('editor escreve uma página completa ... salva, revisão é registrada') — confirm this scope matches TASK-34's publish implementation.
